title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
At a party organized by Royana Drake, a light beam and a mirror reveal a figure with a large beard that Beatrice compares to the rock star Eddie Prescott. Royana explains that Nick and his friend Desmond, an experienced photographer, staged the effect with lighting and costumes to entertain the guests. Following a dinner of various snacks and cakes, the party concludes with a "dragon hunt" featuring a dish of burning grapes on a green tablecloth. As the party ends, Judith notes the need to clean up the mess.

In a London apartment, Hercule Poirot is disappointed when his butler, George, informs him that Solomon Levy cannot visit to debate the Cunningham Road Public Baths murder case due to a severe cold. Poirot receives a subsequent telephone call from his friend, Ariadne Oliver, who sounds breathless and highly agitated. Despite knowing that Mrs. Oliver's visits often involve long sessions of venting grievances, Poirot invites her over and prepares lemon barley tea and white wine for himself.

Mrs. Oliver arrives at the apartment in a state of extreme distress, wearing a soaked fisherman's rain hat and an oilcloth raincoat. She repeatedly expresses a sudden, intense hatred and fear of water, claiming she had never previously considered what water could do. Poirot observes her agitation with interest as George helps her remove her wet clothing.
